subject: Databases
title: Soft Deletes vs Hard Deletes
description: A fast breakdown of Soft Deletes vs Hard Deletes for builders.

hook.text: A user clicks Delete. Do you erase the data forever, or mark it as deleted and keep it? Big decision.

buildup.text: Hard deletes: DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 42. The row is gone. No recovery without a backup.

discovery.text: Soft deletes: UPDATE users SET deleted_at = NOW() WHERE id = 42. The row stays, hidden from normal queries.

twist.text: Soft deletes need a WHERE deleted_at IS NULL filter on every query. Forget it and deleted data reappears.

climax.text: Postgres views or RLS can auto-filter soft deletes. The app never sees deleted rows without special effort.

punchline.text: Soft deletes preserve data. Add automatic filtering to avoid leaks.

quiz.question: What is the biggest risk of soft deletes without proper filtering?
options: Deleted data reappears in queries that forget to check the deleted_at column
Soft-deleted rows take up extra disk space
The database automatically restores soft-deleted rows after 30 days
